svg.title: Primary Research Toolkit — Architecture v3 (curved iteration arrow removed)
svg.desc: 16:9 architecture diagram. Inputs split into project context and firm knowledge. Three sub-skills: two planned stubs and one built (ICS, with Mode 1 and Mode 2). Plugin-shared infrastructure shows scripts, logs, references. Outputs are summary cards and Roll-up; Roll-up hands off to SoF Toolkit Draft mode. Iteration is described in the bottom callout strip.
svg.text: 3 sub-skills · plugin-shared infrastructure · iterative coding rounds → SoF Toolkit handoff

g.text: What the consultant brings in for this engagement
• Analytical frame (issue tree, SOW, kickoff deck)
• Metadata, plus an optional prior Roll-up
Optional prior Roll-up enables cross-round delta (planned)
Newry assets PRT draws on across projects
• Past research archives (prior Roll-ups, pattern library)
Scopes the workstream and drafts the research plan:
branch priorities, interviewee mix and sample size,
interview guides with probing follow-ups and branch
Codes one transcript at a time
Versionable artifacts with READMEs and contracts
Decisions made — per-run, in each output
Recognition-based feedback capture; no per-run prompt
Per-project glossary lives with the project
One record per interview with branch-mapped findings
Cards append to a single project doc as interviews are coded
Strategic verdicts and per-branch findings, with a coverage
table, contradictions, and Outside the Issue Tree items.
Drafts the analytical core that feeds SoF authoring
Roll-up feeds the Summary of Findings drafting workflow
in the SoF Toolkit, a separate Cowork plugin downstream of PRT.
When new interviews come in, ICS is re-run. Mode 1 appends new cards to the project doc, and Mode 2 regenerates the Roll-up against the broader corpus.
Cross-round delta — where Mode 2 reads a prior Roll-up to surface what shifted — is a planned Mode 2 enhancement.
PRT is a Tier 1 / Module 1 plugin in the Newry AI program; SoF Toolkit sits downstream.
